entries.description: Shrugging the bow shoulder up creates tension, reduces stability, and can lead to inconsistent aiming and release.
observable_signs: Bow shoulder visibly elevated towards the ear
Bow arm appears shortened
entries.score_impact: Leads to vertical stringing, inconsistent aim, and potential for premature release due to tension.
entries.embed_text: setup draw bow_shoulder high bow shoulder tension stability vertical stringing inconsistent aim push shoulder down relax shoulder blade drop

entries.description: Hyperextending the bow elbow locks the joint, creating a rigid structure that absorbs shock poorly and can lead to joint injury or string slap. Estimated ranges.
observable_signs: Elbow joint visibly pushed past straight
Arm appears locked
entries.score_impact: Can cause string slap, inconsistent bow torque, and potential for elbow injury, leading to erratic arrow flight.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim bow_elbow bow_arm_extension_angle hyperextension string slap joint injury soft elbow micro-bend erratic arrow flight

entries.description: A significantly bent bow arm reduces the effective draw length, creates instability, and makes it harder to align the shot consistently. Estimated ranges.
observable_signs: Elbow joint visibly bent
Bow arm appears shortened
Bow hand closer to the body
entries.score_impact: Reduces power, causes inconsistent draw length, and leads to horizontal stringing or low shots.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim bow_elbow bow_arm_extension_angle bent bow arm reduced draw length instability horizontal stringing straighten bow arm push through bow hand

entries.description: Squeezing the bow causes torque, which twists the bow and leads to inconsistent arrow flight and poor follow-through.
observable_signs: Fingers tightly wrapped around the grip
Knuckles white
Wrist appears stiff or torqued
coaching_cues: Let the bow sit in your palm
Use a sling, not a death grip
entries.score_impact: Major cause of horizontal stringing and inconsistent left/right arrow placement.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim release bow_wrist bow_hand excessive bow grip pressure torque inconsistent arrow flight horizontal stringing relax bow hand fingers loose

entries.description: Bending or flicking the draw wrist introduces inconsistency in the string release, causing torque and affecting arrow flight.
observable_signs: Draw wrist visibly bent inwards or outwards
Fingers not aligned with the forearm
Hand appears 'flicked'
coaching_cues: Let the string pull your fingers
entries.score_impact: Leads to inconsistent horizontal grouping, causing arrows to fly left or right of the target.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor aim draw_wrist draw_hand bent draw wrist flicked wrist inconsistent release horizontal grouping straight wrist relax wrist

entries.description: A draw elbow that is too high breaks the alignment of the drawing arm and shoulder, preventing proper back tension engagement and creating an unstable anchor.
observable_signs: Draw elbow visibly above the line of the arrow
Draw shoulder elevated
coaching_cues: Elbow in line with the arrow
entries.score_impact: Reduces power, causes inconsistent anchor, and leads to vertical stringing or high shots.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor aim draw_elbow draw_shoulder high draw elbow back tension unstable anchor vertical stringing drop elbow elbow in line with arrow

entries.description: A draw elbow that is too low prevents full engagement of the back muscles, leading to a weak draw, inconsistent anchor, and reliance on arm muscles.
observable_signs: Draw elbow visibly below the line of the arrow
Draw shoulder appears rotated forward
coaching_cues: Elbow in line with the arrow
entries.score_impact: Reduces power, causes inconsistent anchor, and leads to vertical stringing or low shots.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor aim draw_elbow draw_shoulder low draw elbow weak draw inconsistent anchor vertical stringing lift elbow elbow in line with arrow

entries.description: Relying on arm muscles (biceps) to draw the bow leads to fatigue, instability, and an inability to achieve a consistent, powerful release.
observable_signs: Draw elbow collapses or moves forward
Draw shoulder elevates
Visible tension in the bicep
Lack of movement in the shoulder blade
coaching_cues: Think about pulling your elbow back
entries.score_impact: Causes inconsistent draw length, poor holding, and erratic arrow flight due to arm fatigue and inconsistent release.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor aim draw_shoulder spine back tension arm drawing fatigue instability erratic arrow flight pull with back squeeze shoulder blades

entries.description: Allowing the draw shoulder to move forward or 'creep' during the aim phase reduces draw length and back tension, leading to a weak and inconsistent release.
observable_signs: Draw elbow moves forward
Draw shoulder visibly shifts towards the target
String hand moves away from anchor point
coaching_cues: Don't let your elbow creep forward
entries.score_impact: Major cause of low shots and inconsistent vertical grouping due to reduced power and varying draw length.
entries.embed_text: aim draw_shoulder draw_elbow draw shoulder collapse creep reduced draw length weak release low shots inconsistent vertical grouping hold back tension

entries.description: Tilting the head changes the anchor point and eye alignment, leading to inconsistent aiming and difficulty in maintaining a stable sight picture. Estimated ranges.
root_causes: Trying to get a better sight picture
observable_signs: Head visibly tilted to the side (towards or away from the string)
Chin not level
entries.score_impact: Causes inconsistent vertical and horizontal grouping due to a shifting anchor and sight picture.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim head spine head_tilt head tilt inconsistent aiming unstable sight picture head level chin parallel

entries.description: Leaning excessively forward shifts the archer's center of gravity, reducing stability and making it harder to maintain a consistent hold and follow-through. Estimated ranges.
root_causes: Trying to compensate for draw weight
observable_signs: Torso visibly angled forward from the hips
Archer appears to be 'falling' into the shot
entries.score_impact: Leads to inconsistent vertical grouping, especially low shots, and difficulty in holding steady.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim spine hip knee spine_forward_lean excessive forward lean reduced stability inconsistent vertical grouping stand tall engage core vertical spine

entries.description: Tilting the spine laterally shifts the archer's center of gravity and changes the alignment of the bow and draw arm, leading to inconsistent aiming. Estimated ranges.
observable_signs: Torso visibly angled to the left or right
One shoulder significantly higher than the other (not due to draw, but body tilt)
entries.score_impact: Causes inconsistent horizontal grouping and difficulty in finding a consistent anchor point.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim spine hip spine_lateral_tilt lateral spine tilt inconsistent aiming horizontal grouping shoulders level stand straight

entries.description: Shrugging the draw shoulder upwards during the draw creates tension, inhibits proper back muscle engagement, and leads to an unstable anchor and inconsistent release. Estimated ranges.
observable_signs: Draw shoulder visibly elevates towards the ear as the string is pulled
Neck appears shortened
coaching_cues: Pull with your back, not your shoulder
entries.score_impact: Causes inconsistent vertical grouping, high shots, and premature release due to tension.
entries.embed_text: draw draw_shoulder draw_shoulder_rise excessive draw shoulder rise tension unstable anchor inconsistent release vertical grouping shoulder down relax neck pull with back

entries.description: Variations in the anchor point directly change the archer's sight picture and draw length, making consistent aiming impossible. Estimated ranges.
observable_signs: Draw hand position varies relative to the jaw/chin across multiple shots
Head position changes
coaching_cues: Feel the same spot every time
entries.score_impact: The primary cause of inconsistent grouping, both vertical and horizontal, making accurate shooting impossible.
entries.embed_text: anchor draw_hand head anchor_point_consistency inconsistent anchor point sight picture draw length inconsistent grouping feel same spot solid contact
